_meta.description: Hand-labeled ground truth for the 15 internal queue items. Labeled against the same priority/needs_ryan rubric as external emails so cross-source comparison is apples-to-apples.
labeling_principles: An internal escalation that blocks others' work is generally higher priority than a routine external inquiry.
needs_ryan=true for any decision only Ryan can make (build-vs-buy, pricing exceptions, board responses, offer signoffs).
Internal FYIs and 'no decisions needed' updates score low even though they're from team members.

labels.notes: Build-vs-buy on core infra. Only Ryan can call. Blocks sprint planning for the whole eng team. Time-sensitive (today).
Pricing exception that ties to a strategic prospect's RFP. EOD deadline. Ryan must own the call.
BDR lead asking for feedback on a template. Important for team velocity but not urgent. Wednesday deadline.

Customer churn risk escalation — same customer that just emailed externally (Lisa Huang at MountainPeak). The internal and external items should be linked. Only Ryan can authorize an eng resource reallocation.
Board member asking substantive questions before the board meeting. Ryan must respond personally.
Runway / fundraise strategy question. Not today-urgent but Ryan-only decision.
Senior hire with competing offer; Ryan needs to do a closer call. Friday deadline. Top-tier candidate. Cannot be delegated.
Eng FYI on a fix that's already shipped. Good signal but no action needed.
Product lead flagging a real pattern in customer asks. Worth Ryan's attention but no decision today — book the meeting.
Direct report asking for a career/role conversation. Could be a flight-risk signal. Ryan should respond personally to schedule promptly.
BDR asking for pricing guidance ahead of a demo. Time-bound (tomorrow). Ryan should weigh in but could also delegate trust to the AE.
Offer letter awaiting signature. Quick action but only Ryan can sign. EOD deadline.
Small infra decision. Eng lead expressed a preference; Ryan could just rubber-stamp. Not blocking.
Demo prep question with a clear product/commitment tradeoff. Tomorrow deadline. Ryan should weigh in.
Financial FYI with no decisions requested. Useful context but no action.
